questions.question: Write a MongoDB query to find the index(es) of a specific city in a collection, assuming the city is stored in a field called 'city'. Log the result in a variable called 'indexResult'.
questions.hint: Use the explain() method to get the indexes

questions.question: A MongoDB collection has the following indexes: { city: 1 }, { name: 1, age: -1 }. Find the index that contains the 'city' field and log its index specification.
questions.hint: Review the indexes and find the one containing 'city'

questions.question: Explain why MongoDB does not create indexes on all fields when a collection is created. Provide a code example to illustrate this.
questions.hint: Review MongoDB documentation and consider the implications of not creating indexes
